docs.content:
# Biotech / Life-Science Desk — Sector Scan
**Date:** July 6, 2026 — as-of scan, findings are evidence-first. One catalyst (IONS PDUFA) may already be resolved: the olezarsen sHTG FDA action date of June 30, 2026 passed four days ago. Search-source outcomes post-date my latest fetch. Flag below.

---

## Executive Summary

**Desk recommendation: Increase coverage.** There are currently 3–4 verifiable public candidates with differentiated theses and documented runway, plus one high-upside binary bet with a floor in net cash. Avoid pure promise-stage names without clinical data. The obesity/metabolic cluster is modern-day crowded-trade territory (193+ assets in development, LLY already a $1T+ market cap); the interesting edge is in oral small-molecule GLP-1 differentiation and first-in-class mechanism alternatives.

---

## CANDIDATES

### 1. Ionis Pharmaceuticals (IONS) — Platform RNA / Antisense
**Category:** Repeatable platform / near-term compounder (if June 30 PDUFA was positive)

| Factor | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Catalyst** | olezarsen (TRYNGOLZA / sNDA) PDUFA date June 30, 2026 for severe hypertriglyceridemia; peak sales raised to **>$3B** from $2B; zilganersen NDA also accepted Priority Review for Alexander disease |
| **Evidence quality** | High. Four independent commercial launches in 18 months; Q1 2026 revenue $246M (+87% YoY); full-year 2026 revenue guidance raised to **$875–900M** |
| **Probability of success** | Olezarsen Phase 3 data already positive (85% reduction in pancreatitis events); FDA accepted sNDA for Priority Review. Regulatory probability reasonable if PDUFA was approval. |
| **Cash / dilution risk** | $1.9B cash as of Q1 2026. Convertible notes repaid April 1, 2026 ($633M). Targets cash-flow breakeven **2028**. |
| **Valuation** | ~$73–$130 range cited across analysts; market cap ~$12.2B; P/S ~11.4x (vs. 5.3x "fair ratio," fellow Simply Wall St data). |
| **Thesis-breaker / downsides** | **June 30 PDUFA was four days ago — outcome unconfirmed in this source set.** Verify approval letter vs. CRL first. Biogen's SPINRAZA royalties still ~18% of revenue; Arrowhead litigation risk on '333 patent; label restrictions could deflate $2B sHTG market sizing; long-term TRYNGOLZA pricing is $40K WAC (discounted from $595K). |

**Verdict:** Potentially one of the highest-confidence names in the desk's window. Requires PDUFA outcome verification before call decision.

---

### 2. Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX) — AI-Native End-to-End Drug Discovery Platform
**Category:** Repeatable platform compounder (structural, not binary)

| Factor | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Catalyst** | REC-4881 (FAP) FDA engagement update H2 2026 — the first regulatory pathway signal for an internally AI-designed asset; REC-1245 (RBM39 degrader) DAHLIA Phase 1 data; REC-4539 (LSD1i) first patient dosed |
| **Evidence quality** | Solid. $500M+ in pharma partner cash received; 10+ milestones hit; cash burn down ~35% YoY; partnership revenue of $30M (Roche) + $7M (Sanofi) in 2025. |
| **Probability of success** | FDA engagement de-risking over 12–24 months. Platform claim is already partially validated (paying pharma). Approval probability for any one asset still <20% (typical early-stage biotech), but platform value compounds even if individual assets fail. |
| **Cash / dilution risk** | $665M cash (Q1 2026); cash runway confirmed to **early 2028**; no additional financing required per guidance. Low near-term dilution risk. |
| **Valuation** | ~$3.20–$3.80/share, market cap ~$1.72B, EV ~$1.38B (after $665M cash). Described by AInvest as "pricing in a lot more doubt" than Dyne at similar stage. Compare to Dyne's $3B EV vs. 2 programs. |
| **Thesis-breaker / downsides** | Cash-burn improvement fails to hold in next two quarters → 2028 runway guidance loses credibility. REC-4881 regulatory pathway rejected. Exscientia integration fails. Revenue miss remains — Q1 2026 revenue only $6.47M. |

**Verdict:** The most structurally interesting mispriced name across the entire desk scan. Paid partners ($500M+) are the hard validation independent of any drug success. Pure platform-style exposure. **Best asymmetric candidate in the AI drug discovery bucket.**

---

### 3. Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) — In Vivo CRISPR Gene Editing
**Category:** Platform with imminent inflection (regulated product launch expected)

| Factor | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Catalyst** | lonvo-z (NTLA-2002) Phase 3 HAELO: **met all primary and secondary endpoints** (87% attack reduction, freedom from ongoing therapy for most); rolling BLA submission started April 27, 2026; anticipates launch H1 2027. Nex-z Phase 3 restarted after FDA clinical hold lifted. |
| **Evidence quality** | Highest of all gene-editing names searched. Quality clinical data, in vivo systemic delivery proven, BLA rolling. |
| **Probability of success** | Phase 3 passed. FDA acceptance of rolling BLA is positive signal. Commercial launch H1 2027 trajectory plausible. |
| **Cash / dilution risk** | $517M cash + $180M April 2026 equity raise (highly dilutive per Simply Wall St analysis). Runway described as "into 2028 and beyond" but recent equity raise signals cash pressure. |
| **Valuation** | ~$13–$14/share post-drop; Simply Wall St narrative fair value $26.70 (95% upside thesis). P/S ~27.7x vs. peer 10.9x — extremely expensive on sales. |
| **Thesis-breaker / downsides** | Safety signal re-emerges in long-term follow-up; payer pushback on one-time gene editing pricing; nex-z clinical hold re-occurs; recurring equity raises continue to dilute. Simply Wall St valuation gap is wide and vulnerable to sentiment shifts. |

**Verdict:** Strongest clinical evidence in the gene-editing cohort, but valuation and recent dilution are structural headwinds. Position sizing must reflect binary regulatory/commercial risk on lonvo-z launch. Not a platform compounder in the same sense as Ionis — heavily dependent on file/approval trajectory.

---

### 4. Twist Bioscience (TWST) — Synthetic Biology Tools / Diagnostics
**Category:** Repeatable platform / compounder (tools, no clinical binary risk)

| Factor | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Catalyst** | FY2026 revenue guidance raised to $442–447M (+17–19%); Amazon Bio Discovery wet lab partner; $114–115M Q4 FY2026 guidance; adj EBITDA breakeven targeted Q4 FY2026 |
| **Evidence quality** | High. 13 consecutive quarters of sequential growth; beat guidance Q2 FY2026 ($110.7M); Diagnostics segment up 28% YoY. |
| **Probability of success** | EBITDA breakeven target is within 1–2 quarters. High-margin DNA synthesis is a durable structural model. |
| **Cash / dilution risk** | ~$172M cash (Q1 FY2026); adj EBITDA breakeven path means material cash burn reduction ahead. Runway adequate for 2–3 quarters at current trajectory before needing to raise; breakeven changes this calculus. |
| **Valuation** | Not a multiple story yet (pre-profitability). Compounding tools model. |
| **Thesis-breaker / downsides** | Amazon Bio Discovery partnership delivers less than expected; biopharma capex cuts hit NGS assay demand; adj EBITDA breakeven slips past Q4 FY2026; cash goes below $100M triggering a raise. |

**Verdict:** Cleanest "tools compounding" exposure in the scan. No clinical binary, clear path to profitability. Deserves increased desk attention.

---

### 5. Viking Therapeutics (VKTX) — Dual GLP-1/GIP Metabolic Platform (Binary / M&A Optionality)
**Category:** Binary biotech with M&A catalytic overlay

| Factor | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Catalyst** | VK2735 pivotal Phase 3 (VANQUISH-1/2 78-week data mid-2027); interim data late 2026; oral formulation as differentiator; M&A window estimated 55–60% probability |
| **Evidence quality** | Moderate. Phase 2: 14–15% weight loss in 13 weeks vs. Wegovy/Mounjaro peer performance. Comparable tolerability. Oral formulation is real differentiator. |
| **Probability of success** | Dual-agonist obesity space crowded and expensive. Success requires 2027 data to be clearly competitive with Lilly/Novo. |
| **Cash / dilution risk** | Reports indicate cash reserves "support full Phase 3 execution without immediate dilution." Specific dollar amount requires fresh 10-Q. |
| **Valuation** | Market cap cited >$3B+. Acquired premium possibilities at $8–12B if data positive (per analysis). |
| **Thesis-breaker / downsides** | Phase 3 readout underwhelms vs. competitors or shows tolerability issues; oral bioavailability underperforms; Lilly/Novo pricing pressure wipes out margin in a crowded field. |

**Verdict:** Binary obesity/metabolic name with an M&A overlay. High-upside, high-risk. Fundable thesis requires confidence in 2027 dual-agonist readout. Position sizing should reflect binary nature.

---

### 6. Precision BioSciences (DTIL) — Below-Cash Binary Gene Editing
**Category:** Pure binary gambit with a hard net-cash floor

| Factor | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Catalyst** | PBGENE-HBV Phase 1/2a data H1 2026; PBGENE-DMD initial data year-end 2026. ARCUS platform (staggered cuts, single-component) is technically differentiated. |
| **Evidence quality** | Low-to-moderate. ARCUS mechanism is scientifically sound but comparative advantage vs. CRISPR not yet proven in humans. |
| **Probability of success** | Low (~15–20%). Both shots must land. Failure on either "likely renders platform non-competitive" per market participants. |
| **Cash / dilution risk** | Net cash of ~$110.8M — floor. Runway through 2028 at current burn. Near-term dilution low. |
| **Valuation** | Trades below net cash → hard downside limited to cash per share minus burn. Upside 2–5x if both programs validate. |
| **Thesis-breaker / downsides** | Phase 1/2a data failure in HBV; DMD data failure; CRISPR competitors (Editas, Intellia, Prime Medicine) pull ahead; platform not demonstrably better than existing Cas9/Cas12a editing. |

**Verdict:** Acceptable as a small-size, low-conviction allocation for asymmetric upside with a visible hard floor (cash). Not a platform compounder. Ticket size should reflect binary risk.

---

### 7. Agilent Technologies (A) — Life-Science Tools / Diagnostics
**Category:** Repeatable compounder (tools, not biotech)

| Factor | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Catalyst** | FY2026 revenue guidance raised to $7.39–7.49B (+6–8%); LDG segment +12% reported, +9% core in Q2; replacement cycle and new platforms (9500 ICP-MS) driving growth |
| **Evidence quality** | High. Operating margins expanding; double-digit growth in LDG; consistent execution across all end markets. |
| **Probability of success** | Very high. Slow, durable growth model. |
| **Cash / dilution risk** | Low. Mature balance sheet, diversified revenue streams. |
| **Valuation** | Premium P/E relative to broader medtech, but appropriate for quality. Not a multiple-expansion story — priced for steady execution. |
| **Thesis-breaker / downsides** | Academic/large-pharma capex slowdowns; biotech funding freeze extends; instrument replacement cycle stalls. |

**Verdict:** Reliable tools compounder, lower alpha but lower risk. Good portfolio anchor; reduces overall portfolio volatility if paired with higher-beta peers (Recursion, NTLA).

---

## OBESITY / METABOLIC — Thematic Positioning

| Name | Status | Note |
|---|---|---|
| Viking (VKTX) | Pivotal Phase 3 | Best asymmetric name if oral Readout surprises |
| Structure Therapeutics (FRLX) | Phase 3 enrollment H2 2026 | Pre-revenue, $1.4B cash, but crowded field |
| Kailera (KRT) | IPO'd at $625M raise | No Phase 3 data until 2028; too far out for this desk window |
| Eli Lilly / Novo Nordisk | Incumbents | Crowded trade, already mega-cap |

**Verdict for desk:** The obesity-metabolic cluster is **highly crowded and mostly priced for large-pharma Winners.** The only asymmetric entry points are names at the binary Phase 3 readout window (VKTX) or at meaningful discounts to their net-cash floors (DTIL). Space does not warrant increased broad coverage — reduce to catalyst-driven specific names only.

---

## GENE EDITING — Thematic Positioning

| Name | Stage | Note |
|---|---|---|
| Intellia (NTLA) | Commercial launch 2027 | Best-in-class evidence, expensive, recently diluted |
| Editas (EDIT) | Preclinical → clinical | EDIT-401 compelling; CB/CVC patent resolved in favor; pre-clinical only |
| Precision BioSciences (DTIL) | Phase 1/2 | Binary, below net cash floor |
| CRISPR Therapeutics (CRSP) | CASGEVY approved | Now a late-stage commercial compounder with SyNTase pipeline |

**Verdict:** Gene editing offers the strongest pipeline depth across public names. Ionis's antisense platform and Intellia's in vivo CRISR are the two highest-confidence theses. Editas is a lower-conviction but interesting preclinical bet on a different editase (Cas12a).

---

## DISPATCH: BINARY vs. PLATFORM SEPARATION

| Binary / Gambit | Platform / Compounder |
|---|---|
| Precision BioSciences (DTIL) | Ionis (IONS) |
| Viking (VKTX) | Recursion (RXRX) |
| BioAge Labs (BIOA)* | Twist Bioscience (TWST) |
| Structure (FRLX) | Agilent (A) |

*BIOA is too pre-revenue / preclinical to fit either cleanly — requires Phase 2a data H1 2026 (possibly out now) before it graduates.

---

## RECOMMENDATION

| Action | Name | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Increase priority desk coverage** | **RXRX** | $665M cash, validated pharma engine, EV compression, low-burn runway to 2028 catalysts, mispriced vs. Dyne precedent |
| **Increase priority** | **TWST** | Path to EBITDA breakeven within 12 months; tools compounding; no clinical binary; Amazon partnership validates reach |
| **Increase priority, verify PDUFA first** | **IONS** | If olezarsen approved June 30, 2026, upgrade to highest-conviction RNA platform name; monitor Arrowhead litigation and Biogen royalty concentration |
| **Maintain** | **NTLA** | Strongest clinical evidence in gene editing, high confidence on lonvo-z BLA trajectory, but recent dilution and 27x P/S demand larger position buffer and timing entry |
| **Reduce / catalysts-only** | Obesity/metabolic cluster (VKTX, FRLX, KRT) | Crowded trade barring a clear mispricing entry |
| **Reduce** | Pure longevity pre-clinical | Altos Labs is private; BIOA still pre-Phase 2; insufficient public leverage |
| **Silent / skip** | CVRs, warrants, below-$50M micro-cap flags outside DTIL | Limited institutional overwatch, unreliable filings |

---

## KNOWN GAP — VERIFY BEFORE ACTING

The **IONS olezarsen PDUFA action date of June 30, 2026 resolved four days ago** (as of July 6, 2026). The search-source trajectory does not yet reflect the FDA's letter (approval, CRL, or delay). All upside/side estimates above assume approval. If a CRL or restrictive label came back, the $12B market cap thesis compresses materially — particularly given Arrowhead patent litigation tail risk and the $40K WAC discount trade-off.

**Recommended action before sizing IONS:** Pull FDA announcement or press release on olezarsen / TRYNGOLZA sHTG supplemental NDA from June 30/July 1, 2026.

---

*End of desk scan. No further questions — full autonomous deliverable.*

# Leemer Investment Research — Desk Management Memo
**Date:** July 6, 2026  
**To:** Research desk manager / chief of staff  
**From:** Gus / Hermes Agent  
**Re:** Specialist desk evaluation — hire/fire/reweight + meta-research queue

---

## Executive Summary

**The desk is overfit to AI infrastructure optics and semis, underfit to the power/grid bottleneck, and wasting cycles on social-media rumor mills.** Three desks are earning their keep (Power/Grid, Source Intelligence, AI Apps/Defense). Two are structurally stale (Optical Networking if it keeps anchoring on AAOI; Biotech if it keeps treating gene-editing binaries as compounders). One support function (Hyperscaler Capex) is useful but should be subordinated to the power/grid desk. **Two new desks should be created:** Advanced Nuclear / Fuel Cycle and AI Infrastructure Credit / Underwriting.

---

## Desk-by-Desk Evaluation

### Tier 1 — Earn Weight, Scale Headcount

**1. Power / Grid / Data-Center Infrastructure**
- **Unique alpha:** Verifiable backlogs (PWR $48.5B, GEV $150B+, VRT $15B, ETN >$19B), pricing power, transformer lead times 80–144 weeks, interconnection queues 4–7+ years. This is the least crowded, most evidence-rich AI-adjacent basket.
- **Evidence quality:** Primary-source filings, confirmed order language, backlog-to-revenue math. Not rumor-dependent.
- **Alpha potential:** Highest in the firm. Less followed than AAOI/semis because it looks "boring industrial."
- **Action:** **UPGRADE TO FULL DESK (2 analysts).** This should be the largest sleeve after AI apps.

**2. AI Apps / Autonomy / Defense**
- **Unique alpha:** PLTR ($1B+ quarter, 85% growth, 128% NDR), PANW (AI security revenue re-acceleration), KTOS (defense procurement, autonomous drones). Verified execution, not narrative.
- **Evidence quality:** Earnings, contract announcements, DoD budget linkage. Strong.
- **Alpha potential:** High, but PLTR/PANW are becoming recognized; KTOS remains underfollowed.
- **Action:** **HOLD WEIGHT, expand defense procurement tracking.** Add DoD budget cycle and Replicator/autonomous-munitions milestones to catalyst calendar.

**3. Source Intelligence / Funds Social**
- **Unique alpha:** SemiAnalysis is the desk's highest-signal open-source feed; Situational Awareness LP 13F debunked the "everyone is long AI infra" narrative (revealing paired long/short options book). Social/X is mostly noise.
- **Evidence quality:** Mixed — SemiAnalysis is primary; 13F is official but lagged; social accounts (@aleabitoreddit, Serenity) have produced zero verified filings in recent sweeps.
- **Alpha potential:** Medium-high if sourcing discipline tightens; low if social rumor keeps getting treated as evidence.
- **Action:** **HOLD, but implement evidence-tier scoring and downgrade social leads to "rumor only."** SemiAnalysis remains the only social-grade source worth daily reads.

---

### Tier 2 — Maintain with Corrections

**4. Semiconductors / HBM / Accelerators (TSM, MU, AVGO, MRVL, ASML)**
- **Unique alpha:** Bottleneck migration from silicon to CoWoS advanced packaging; HBM4 cycle risk in 2027; TSM scarcity rent; MU best-positioned memory name.
- **Evidence quality:** Strong on TSM CoWoS, MU HBM4; weaker on forward pricing.
- **Alpha potential:** Medium. Crowding risk is rising; 13F shows major AI funds hedging mega-cap semis via options, not just longing them.
- **Action:** **HOLD WEIGHT, but pivot focus from "who sells the most chips" to "who holds the packaging/memory chokepoint."** Reduce mega-cap NVDA/AVGO narrative exposure; increase TSM/MU/ASML (with China overhang flag) granularity.

**5. Hyperscaler Capex / Financing / AI Demand**
- **Unique alpha:** Identified the "in-between layer" financing fragility (CoreWeave debt, Nebius leases, project-finance retrenchment). Useful cross-desk coordination.
- **Evidence quality:** Good on public hyperscaler capex (~$725B combined 2026); weaker on private-operator utilization without direct lender data.
- **Alpha potential:** Medium. Best as a **support function, not a standalone sleeve.** Power/grid and optical desks should consume this output.
- **Action:** **SUBORDINATE to Power/Grid desk.** One analyst covering both capex demand and financing stress is sufficient.

---

### Tier 3 — Stale or Overfit; Correct or Wind Down

**6. Optical / AI Networking**
- **Unique alpha:** 800G/1.6T demand real; CPO shifting to revenue. But the desk's focus on AAOI as a *compounder* is wrong.
- **Evidence quality:** Strong on LITE/COHR lasers and AVGO/ANET switching; weak on AAOI's margin/dilution trajectory being acknowledged.
- **Alpha potential:** **Collapsing for AAOI-specific ideas.** Customer concentration 98%, share count +60% in a year, GAAP GM 29% and falling. AAOI is a trade, not a sleeve anchor.
- **Action:** **REWEIGHT — shift optical desk mandate from "module assemblers" to chokepoint lasers (LITE, COHR), DSPs (MRVL, AVGO), and switching fabrics (ANET, CRDO).** If the desk insists on AAOI, cap it at satellite size and require quarterly margin-inflection proof.

**7. Biotech / Health Longevity / Life-Science Tools**
- **Unique alpha:** IONS platform RNA strong if PDUFA was positive; RXRX most interesting mispriced AI drug discovery name; NTLA strongest gene-editing clinical but with valuation/dilution wreckers.
- **Evidence quality:** Mixed. Clinical data is primary; market sizing and runway claims need refreshing.
- **Alpha potential:** Medium-low for the desk's stated scope. Obesity/metabolic is a crowded trade. Gene editing is binary, not compounding.
- **Action:** **REWEIGHT — drop obesity/gene-editing binary coverage. Focus on platform biotech (IONS, RXRX) and life-science tools (TWST) with real revenue and cash flow. Hire one specialist with clinical-trials diligence fluency, not hype detection.**

---

## Hire / Fire / Reweight Summary

| Desk | Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Power / Grid / DC Infrastructure | **HIRE (2nd analyst)** | Strongest asymmetry, least crowded, primary evidence |
| AI Apps / Autonomy / Defense | **HOLD + expand DoD tracking** | PLTR/PANW verified; KTOS underfollowed |
| Source Intelligence | **HOLD, discipline sourcing** | SemiAnalysis gold; social leads are noise |
| Semis / HBM / Accelerators | **HOLD, pivot to chokepoints** | TSM/MU/ASML valid; reduce mega-cap narrative |
| Hyperscaler Capex / Financing | **SUBORDINATE to Power/Grid** | Useful support, not standalone alpha |
| Optical / AI Networking | **REWEIGHT chokepoint focus** | AAOI is crowded/trade-only |
| Biotech / Health Longevity | **REWEIGHT or FIRE if no pivot** | Current scope is crowded/binary; needs platform/tools refocus |

---

## New Desks to Create

**1. Advanced Nuclear / Fuel Cycle Specialist**
- **Why:** LEU ($3.8B backlog, $900M HALEU task order), BWXT ($7.3B backlog, sole navy/SMR pressure vessel supplier), OKLO (space nuclear LOI with Centrus, Meta pre-payment). April 2026 White House Space Nuclear Mandate is a structural catalyst.
- **Scope:** Fuel cycle (LEU), naval/SMR pressure vessels (BWXT), reactor developers (OKLO, NuScale). Overlaps with Power/Grid but distinct regulatory/commercial cycle.
- **Alpha potential:** Medium-high. LEU is grounded; OKLO is binary but with federal tailwind.

**2. AI Infrastructure Credit / Underwriting**
- **Why:** Hyperscaler capex desk flagged project-finance retrenchment around CoreWeave-linked Pennsylvania capacity. The "in-between layer" (GPU co-lo, neoclouds, project-financed builds) is where financing stress will surface first.
- **Scope:** Leverage/lease structures of CoreWeave, Nebius, Crusoe, Lambda, etc.; project-finance lender exposure; utilization underwriting assumptions.
- **Alpha potential:** High for short-side / credit-style ideas; low for long-only public equities (most are private). Could be fused with Hyperscaler Capex desk as a half-time credit analyst.

---

## Stale / Overfit Coverage to Kill or Demote

1. **AAOI as a compounder.** The desk has not updated its mental model from "AI optics winner" to "crowded module assembler with margin compression and dilution." Every specialist note flags this; yet the hypergrowth scout still lists optics as a primary sleeve. **Kill the anchor, retain only as a tactical trade.**
2. **Obesity/metabolic biotech.** 193+ assets in development, LLY already $1T+. The edge is not in GLP-1 me-toos; it is in oral small-molecule differentiation. Current biotech output doesn't show that filter.
3. **Gene-editing binaries as platform compounds.** NTLA is clinical-validated but $180M equity raise in April 2026 destroyed the "compounder" thesis. Treat as a regulated-product launch trade, not a durable business.
4. **Social/X lead generation treated as evidence.** @aleabitoreddit/Serenity, @damnnang2, @conflict_radar, @marcusmilione have produced zero filing-level corroboration in recent sweeps. They are **rumor generators, not sources.** Downgrade all to "watch only" with explicit verification requirement before any claim reaches the thesis tracker.
5. **"Everyone is long AI infrastructure" narrative.** Situational Awareness LP's Q1 13F shows $13.68B book with 72.66% top-10 concentration, 63% turnover, and *large put/options exposure* to SMH/NVDA/AVGO/AMD. The desk must stop regurgitating the consensus long narrative; the smart money is hedging.

---

## Meta-Research Queue — What Gus Should Build Next

1. **Source Quality Scoring System** — Tier sources by evidence type (filings > transcripts > paid research > open-source specialist > social rumor). Auto-flag claims that lack primary-source corroboration before they reach thesis tracker.
2. **13F / Insider Transaction Parser** — Auto-parse 13F filings for crowding signals, options notional, and concentration changes. Reduce the 45-day lag by surfacing Form 13F-HR and Form 4 filings within 24 hours.
3. **Catalyst Calendar** — PDUFA dates, DOE SMR selections, NERC LTRA releases, hyperscaler earnings, defense budget milestones. Single view across desks.
4. **Position-Sizing Framework** — Correlate desk convictions to sleeve limits. Prevent duplicate AI-demand bets across AAOI, power, and semis.
5. **Power Grid Backlog Tracker** — Real-time (quarterly) backlog conversion math for PWR, GEV, VRT, ETN, Siemens Energy. Book-to-bill, backlog growth, AI-exposure language flagging.
6. **Biotech Platform Filter** — Auto-screen biotech names for cash runway <24 months, dilution history, and Phase 3 readout timing. Flag binary vs. platform characteristics before analyst notes are drafted.

---

## Bottom Line

The desk is not bad, but it is **misdimensioned.** Too many analysts covering crowded AI-infrastructure optics and semis; not enough covering the physical-layer constraint (power/grid) and the emerging credit stress in AI infrastructure financing. Biotech needs a hard reset toward platform/tools or should be paused. Source intelligence is useful only with strict evidence discipline.

**Recommended next-quarter headcount allocation:**
- Power/Grid: 2 analysts (up from 1)
- AI Apps/Defense: 1.5 analysts (up from 1, add DoD procurement)
- Semis/HBM: 1 analyst (down from implicit 2, sharpen to chokepoints)
- Optical Networking: 0.5 analyst (down from 1, lasers/DSPs only)
- Hyperscaler Capex: 0.5 analyst (subordinated to Power/Grid)
- Biotech: 0.5 analyst (pause if no platform/tools pivot in 30 days)
- New: Advanced Nuclear 0.5, AI Infra Credit 0.5

**Total: 6 effective analyst-slots, up from current ~5 but reweighted toward evidence-rich, underfollowed, high-asymmetry areas.**
